creator: gerald@pfeifer.com
text:
On Sun, 4 Feb 2007, Pav Lucistnik wrote:
> This is simple. It's not a tunable - port maintainers are not supposed
> to set it.

Thanks for the explanation!  I was running into problems in some 
situation on the FreeBSD ports build cluster, that's when I stumbled
over this.

Based on your description, may I propose the following patch to portmgr?

(Note: this won't need a full run on the ports cluster. :-)

Gerald

Index: bsd.port.mk
===================================================================
RCS file: /home/pcvs/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk,v
retrieving revision 1.551
diff -u -3 -p -r1.551 bsd.port.mk
--- bsd.port.mk	5 Feb 2007 22:57:20 -0000	1.551
+++ bsd.port.mk	7 Feb 2007 00:27:01 -0000
@@ -1039,6 +1039,15 @@ FreeBSD_MAINTAINER=	portmgr@FreeBSD.org
 # Most port authors should not need to understand anything after this point.
 #
 
+# Variables internally used by ports/Mk.  Do not, I repeat, do not use
+# this in any of your ports!
+#
+# NO_LDCONFIG_MTREE
+#				- Denotes whether the libdata/ldconfig directory is part of
+#				  the mtree on a given OSVERSION system.  If it is not, we
+#				  create the directory, pull in the ldconfig_compat port,
+#				  and clean up on de-installation.
+
 # Look for ${WRKSRC}/.../*.orig files, and (re-)create
 # ${FILEDIR}/patch-* files from them.
